data.title: XRP Maintains Macro Bullish Structure Despite Deeper Correction
data.description:
XRP continues to maintain its macro bullish structure despite experiencing a deeper corrective move than initially anticipated. Although price action has tested lower levels, it has not confirmed a higher-timeframe breakdown, suggesting the pullback is still part of a broader consolidation within an ongoing uptrend rather than a full trend reversal. XRP Dips Deeper, But HTF Level Still Holds In a recent XRP update, Hov noted that price action pushed deeper toward the lows than what would typically be acceptable for the previously considered diagonal scenario. The move forced a reassessment of the short-term structure
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/analysis/xrp/xrp-macro-bullish-structure/

data.title: Microsoft Appoints Asha Sharma to Lead Xbox and Gaming Division
data.description: Microsoft Corp. has announced the appointment of AI executive Asha Sharma as the new head of its Xbox and gaming division, succeeding Phil Spencer. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's renewed focus on console users after years of prioritizing mobile and PC gaming products. Sharma's leadership is expected to steer Microsoft's gaming strategy towards enhancing the console experience, aligning with the company's broader objectives in the gaming industry.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Congo's Central Bank to Acquire Gold for Reserves
data.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo's central bank is set to start acquiring gold for its monetary reserves from a state-owned trader, according to an announcement made on Friday. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic move aimed at bolstering the nation's financial stability. This decision marks a significant step in the country's efforts to diversify its reserve assets and strengthen its economic position. The central bank's initiative reflects a broader trend among nations seeking to enhance their monetary reserves with precious metals, particularly gold, which is often viewed as a safe haven asset. The acquisition process will involve collaboration with the state-owned trader, ensuring a streamlined and efficient approach to increasing the country's gold reserves. This development underscores the importance of gold in global financial systems and its role in supporting national economies.

data.title: Bitcoin Network Activity Falls 50%: On-Chain Data Tells a Different Story
data.description:
Bitcoin’s on-chain activity has dropped sharply since the 2021 peak, yet the underlying structure of the market suggests maturation rather than deterioration. According to data shared by Santiment, daily unique BTC wallets interacting on the network have declined by roughly 42% over the past five years, while new wallet creation has fallen by approximately 47%.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bitcoin-network-activity-falls-50-on-chain-data-tells-a-different-story/

data.title: Gambling's Increasing Influence in Sports Discussed by Randall Williams
data.description: Randall Williams has highlighted the expanding impact of gambling within the sports industry. Bloomberg posted on X, noting that while the sector has faced various scandals, the prevailing argument suggests that ignoring legal gambling could lead to illegal gambling gaining dominance. Williams emphasizes the importance of addressing this issue to prevent illegal activities from overshadowing legitimate operations.

data.title: Apollo Global CEO Discusses Market Liquidity Growth
data.description: Bloomberg posted on X that Apollo Global CEO Marc Rowan shared insights on the evolution of market liquidity. Rowan highlighted the transition from proprietary origination to the expansion of secondary market liquidity. He emphasized the importance of these developments in the financial landscape. Rowan's comments reflect a broader trend in the industry towards enhancing market efficiency and accessibility.

data.title: Supreme Court Reverses Trump Tariffs
data.description:
Stocks and crypto rallied after SCOTUS invalidated Trump's tariff program.
source: https://www.bankless.com/read/news/supreme-court-reverses-trump-tariffs

data.title: Visa's Acquisition of Prosa Blocked by Mexican Antitrust Commission
data.description: Visa's attempt to acquire payment processor Prosa has been rejected by Mexico's National Antitrust Commission. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this as the first significant action by the country's newly established competition authority. The decision marks a notable move in regulating market competition within Mexico's financial sector.

data.title: Amazon's Strategic Shift Towards AI and Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Amazon is making a significant strategic shift towards artificial intelligence and cloud infrastructure, projecting $200 billion in capital expenditures by 2026. According to NS3.AI, this move aims to capitalize on growth in AI, data centers, and technology innovation. Despite a bearish short-term outlook, Amazon's dominance in e-commerce and cloud services, coupled with its strong AI investments, positions it favorably for long-term growth compared to Walmart. Recently, Walmart has closed the gap with Amazon in e-commerce, with its stock outperforming Amazon's by nearly 30% over the past year. However, Amazon's focus on technological advancements suggests a promising future beyond immediate market fluctuations.

data.title: ProShares' Money Market ETF Sees $17 Billion Trading Volume on Launch Day
data.description: ProShares has introduced its money market ETF, IQMM, specifically targeting U.S. stablecoin issuers. According to NS3.AI, this ETF achieved a remarkable $17 billion in trading volume on its first day. This marks the first financial product to align with the U.S. stablecoin regulation act, GENIUS, which has been in effect since July of last year. In comparison, BlackRock's similar stablecoin money market ETF, IBIT, saw $1 billion in trading volume on its launch day in January 2024.

data.title: Bitcoin Core Integrates Cluster Mempool for Enhanced Transaction Management
data.description: Bitcoin Core has integrated a new transaction mempool system known as Cluster Mempool. According to NS3.AI, this redesigned system organizes transactions into clusters and chunks, aligning mempool operations more closely with mining incentives. The upgrade aims to simplify fee estimation, enhance the security of second layer protocols, and optimize block construction by addressing conflicts from previous methods. The new architecture is designed to improve transaction replacement reliability, reduce eviction unpredictability, and support Bitcoin's decentralized system.
